mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This negative effect on S&P 500 forward returns manifests over a 20-day horizon as market reprices earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% in Nov 2015 near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE decline and 20-day forward returns exceeds 0.05 in absolute value, or SPX produces positive returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE falls by > 0.2pp over preceding month.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above trend, signaling robust consumer demand and forward earnings growth. This positive demand signal drives S&P 500 returns upward over a 60-day horizon as equity analysts revise earnings forecasts higher in response to strengthening consumption.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 0.5% in a tightening cycle, signaling monetary policy restraint. Higher discount rates applied to future equity cash flows reduce present valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices risk-free rates upward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% and continues rising for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% in Nov 2015, near zero-lower-bound
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle initiating from near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints. This supply weakness reduces earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as high-frequency traders reprrice cyclical exposure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below 0.2% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns exceeds 0.04 in absolute value, or SPX produces positive returns in > 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, signaling broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. This joint demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days as the market recognizes synchronized economic expansion and upgrades growth forecasts.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 0.4% simultaneously
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS growth) with 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 48% of jointly-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains stable, signaling labor market softening without immediate wage pressure relief. This mixed signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days as the market fears a growth slowdown without offsetting monetary easing.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 100K AND UNRATE change < 0.1pp over same period
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration with labor market softening
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (PAYEMS growth × UNRATE stability) with 60-day returns exceeds 0.05 in absolute value, or SPX produces positive returns in > 55% of jointly-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1%, creating a negative real rate environment. The Federal Reserve's implicit commitment to eventual tightening produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days as the market reprices the terminal rate higher.
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization from zero rates with rising inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real rate gap (CPIAUCSL growth minus FEDFUNDS) with 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or SPX produces positive returns in > 58% of jointly-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling economic slowdown. This demand weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as equity analysts downgrade earnings forecasts in response to slowing growth momentum.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns exceeds 0.04 in absolute value, or SPX produces positive returns in > 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%.
